name: Class Recap — June 25
homework: Review Lesson 5 (て-form and た-form) and prepare your story to share. Next class (7/2): Easy Japanese skits #35 & #36 to practice 〜たい (want to), connecting verbs with て, and た-form + ことがあります.
topics_covered: 主人公 vs 主役 (main character)
Prep: た-form + ことがあります

summary.h: 主人公 vs 主役 — a correction
items: Atsuko corrected last week's term for 'main character.' The better word is 主人公 (しゅじんこう), not 主役 (しゅやく).
主人公 (shujinkou): focuses on the plot — who the story is about (protagonist).
主役 (shuyaku): focuses on the spotlight — who gets the most screen time, billing, or attention (lead role / star).
They overlap in casual speech, but 主人公 is about the narrative and 主役 is about the performance.
〜たい / 〜たいです = 'want to.' u-verbs → い-form + たい; ru-verbs → drop る + たい.
The object of たい usually takes が (何がしたい？), though を is also possible.
Connect two action verbs with the て-form: 大涌谷に行って、黒たまごが食べたいです。
Review: た-form + ことがあります = have done / have the experience of doing something.

groups.name: 主人公 vs 主役 — Main Character
groups.notes: Atsuko corrected the term for 'main character.'

cards.notes: Focuses on the plot — who the story is about.
Focuses on the spotlight — most screen time, billing, or attention.

cards.back: 主人公 = the story's focus; 主役 = the performance's spotlight
cards.notes: Similar meanings, interchangeable casually — but 主人公 = plot, 主役 = attention/role.

groups.name: Prep for 7/2 — Phrases to Practice
groups.notes: For Easy Japanese #35/#36: 〜たい, connecting verbs with て, and た-form + ことがあります.

cards.notes: u-verbs → い-form + たい; ru-verbs → drop る + たい. Object often takes が: 何がしたい？

cards.back: What do you want to do?
cards.notes: が commonly marks the object of たい (を also possible).

cards.back: do A and (then) B — join actions with the て-form
cards.notes: 大涌谷に行って、黒たまごが食べたいです。

cards.back: have done / have the experience of [verb]
cards.notes: Review from 6/18: past (た) form + ことがあります.
